Kevin Richard Martin.title: Above the clouds
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Kevin Richard Martin - Above the clouds
'Above the clouds' is the latest installment in KRM's warped, dubbed out take on ambient Jazz and smudged drone, after his previously self released  'Downtown' and 'Nightcrawler'(Both on the Intercranial Recordings label).  This nocturnal soundtrack for concrete jungles, conjures images of perpetually rainswept conurbations, overcrowded twilight zones and the longest nights spent in neon lit cities no-go zones. It evocatively reflects glass and stone stretching endlessly into the distance, covered in clouds of hallucinatory smog, as this album wraps itself around you unfathomably..
His latest chapter of sin city, jazz noir, is slower, more rarefied and dreamy than the previous two offerings, in all the best senses, as it recalls the bewitching romantic desolation of his other infamous project King Midas Sound(Alongside Roger Robinson).This album memorably captures a sonic aerial view of vast cities, as clouds of deeply reverberent dub chords hang eternally in the air above the befitting boom bap of the thuggishly minimal drum patterns. The narrative remains mysterious and melancholic throughout, but the tone is again irresistibly alluring.
Like a musical translation of Paul Auster's unsettling urban neurosis, or the mesmeric romance of Wong Kar Wei's finest scenes, the mood is pure stealth, continually shifting emotionally, and philosophically, but thankfully, the result's still remain 101% Martin....For example, 'Sleepless', bewilderingly, simultaneously summons, the ghosts of AR Kane, Bohren, Burial and even Goldie's's Metalheadz,  and mad as that idea seems, the resulting track sounds shockingly fresh and spectral, even at 15 minutes long !  Alternately, the title track itself, sounds like a fictitious battle between DJ Krush and Dylan Carlson's EARTH, for who would steal the crown of slowest and lowest, as its hazy, methodone fuelled crawl swings back and forth seductively. And lastly, the limitlessly stretched fade out 'Gone to Heaven', could be the result, if Tim Hecker and The Necks ever jammed an idea through to the early hours.., but very weirdly reminds me of 10cc's 'Im not in love'  !?!?....
Cinematic in scope, panoramic in scale, paranoid in tendency,  'Above the clouds' is the latest evidence of Martin's blossoming craft as both composer and producer, and offers a surprising haven for meditational Jazz addicts, drone metal casualties and noise worshipping shoegazers alike, to get lost in indefinitely..
Cover Photograph : Rikki Chan(Unsplash)
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released February 3, 2023
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Mixed/Mastered : Brussels Zoo 2023
Cover Photograph : Rikki Chan(Unsplash)

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Bedroom Loops
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Kevin Richard Martin's 'Bedroom Loops'. Music composed horizontally, and best listened to horizontally.
KRM sketched the music in seven consecutive nights on his bed, and then subsequently wrote/mixed them, in late evening sessions in the lab.
Seven nocturnal cycles of smokey, hazy, hypnotic repetitions.All sounds composed in the granular realm, incorporating only granular hardware synthesis/sampling/plugins and shadowy, dubbed out fx, and then fittingly chopped/screwed to the most single mindedly, drowsy pace in the analogue world over two months.
Seven ambient variations on the same misty, dreamlike theme...
A glittering,droning, blurred soundset of half heard delirium. An ode to heavy lids, an album to f-ck to, or get f-cked up to, or simply zone out with..
Immersion time. The emphasis is on slower than slow, the glacial shifts are subliminal, the impact is soporific. Anyone hooked on Martin's recent 'White Light/Red Light' releases, or the alien soundscapes found in King Midas Sound's 'Solitude' will enjoy this warm medication.

Mixed : Brussels Zoo

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Black
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Kevin Richard Martin - 'Black'
'Black' is a musical eulogy to Amy Winehouse, a heartfelt memorial to a sorrowful demise. Its an album of predominantly beatless ambience possesed by the ghost of 'Back to black'. Fragmented moods and hypnotic drones melt together, as its circular beauty is set adrift, floating away into an endless void.. where the original only remains in spirit alone...
It doesnt make particular sense why i was drawn to this idea and compelled to immerse myself in the original song, and her life in general, but sometimes you just have to roll with your muse..Having barely registered her whilst she was alive, and not cared for Mark Ronson's poptastic productions, it was only when i heard of her tragic death, that it struck an unexpected chord, recalling the same surreal emotional impact, as when i had heard of Kurt Cobain's premature passing previously.... Both figures were unarguably gifted, but both left this planet largely without essential support, whilst they were at the peak of their powers and on top of the musical world..
Gone too soon, departed too young, a world weary voice, carried on a downward spiral, Amy Winehouse seemed trapped in her self destructive descent... It was only years later, whilst randomly watching Asif Kapadia's moving bio doc 'Amy', on a long distance flight that i realised the scale of her greatness and the tragedy of the circumstances that led to her untimely death...This album is simultaneously a treatise on lovelessness, tragedy and loss, echoing the absence of a support network when it matters most during such a freefall. And just as myself and others i know shed tears watching that 'Amy' documentary, this album is as much a reaction to the universal emotional themes conveyed within that touching documentary as to the tragic life of Amy herself.
I have been workin on the idea of this sonic album for over a year, and the slo-mo dream rotations remain as blurred and impressionistic as they are repetitive and haunting... An eerie cocktail of spectral jazz, shoegaze drone and dubbed out ambient music, it continues the solo path i have been developing as KRM.
I hope it strikes a chord.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released October 18, 2023
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Mixed/Mastered : Brussels Zoo 2023
Ai Amy illustration c/o liberty99 / Pixabay

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Downtown
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Continuing the blunted, narco-jazz swing of KRM's previous 'Nightcrawler' album, 'Downtown' is an even slower, more eerie ride into dread city noir soundscapes and cinematic scale hallucinations.
From the opening drone jazz of 'Berlin Walls', which sounds like Mwandishi era rhythmic gyrations, mixed by Thomas Köner, directly leading to the ominous grind. and low end dirge of 'Stonebridge Park Estate', Martin is again bossing his own paranoid musical terrain, as he soundtracks a concrete jungle's distant noise, buried rhythms and infinite pulsations.
A tense narrative, of his own relentless making, Kevin again obsessively crafts sonic detail, infers doomed narratives, and dubs out the metropolis unforgettably....Anyone fiending for an imaginary meeting between Cecil McBee, Mad Professor and Bernard Herrmann, will surely lap up this journey into a beautiful unknown.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released October 7, 2022
Composed / Produced / Mastered - Kevin Richard Martin (Brussels Zoo - 2022)
Photography / Design : The Pathological Puppy
Copyright Control 2022

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Frequencies for Leaving Earth - Vol.1
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Kevin Richard Martin(The Bug/King Midas Sound/ Zonal), today launches his own digital label, Intercranial Recordings, with the simultaneous, twin release of ‘Frequencies for Leaving Earth Vol.1’ and ‘Frequencies for Leaving Earth Vol.2’. Both volumes were composed, mixed and produced during the lockdown period. Kevin spent this surreal, intense time, rebuilding his studio and retreating into these alternate sonic realities…
Volume 1 is epically slow. Three graceful tracks that are as beautiful as they are spiritual, existing in a glacial twilight zone, floating between drone, neo-classical, and extremely slo-mo jazz. Sounding like a score for a lost Tarkovsky film, the cavernous spaces between the notes are as seductive as the lush music within the album. It ’s three lengthy pieces are three variations on one haunting, melody..
Alternately Volume 2 is its inorganic opposite, Written for Shepard tone, mellotron and sub bass. This second volume, is far more austere, alien and foreboding, revelling in its praise of the shepard tone and Kevin Martin’s ongoing obsession with scarce sci-fi scores.
The producer ventures further out sonically, into an extra terrestrial deep listening experience, that is fuelled on massive low end gravity and hypnotic tension, but retains a rich vein of soul amidst the tripped, electronic machinery.
It echoes disc two of Techno Animal’s ‘Re-entry’, the spectral ambience of his debut album ’Sirens’ on Lawrence English Room40 label, and at times even sounds like the heaviest dubs applied to an updated BBC Radiophonic Workshop, by a latter day Autechre..
Yup, its a trip.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released June 5, 2020
Written/Mixed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Mastered : Lawrence English
Artwork : Pedro Maia

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Frequencies For Leaving Earth - Vol.3
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Following on from last month's twin releases, on his freshly launched Intercranial Recordings imprint, Kevin Richard Martin returns with another enchanting voyage into deep space.
Veering between hypnotic sedatives and tsunami like tidal flows, the warmth of Volume 3 is tangible, and the melodic undercurrents are irresistible..Setting church organs adrift on a sea of sub bass meditations, there is a captivating glow within these 7 molten movements, as they oscillate between breathtaking tension and goosebump release, throughout the profane explorations…. .
When Kevin jokes its “my gospel album”, it puts a smile on my face, but once fully immersed in these songs of praise and loss, who am i to disagree ….?
Undoubtedly Volume 3 contains some of the most beautiful pieces of music Martin has ever released.
In places sounding like a a dream fusion of AFXs lushest melodies with Stars of the Lid’s most seductive sonic hymns, at other times sounding like Spacemen 3 stretched to infinity, the mood conveyed within this record is overwhelming.
Simply gorgeous.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released July 2, 2020
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Frequencies For Leaving Earth Vol.4
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
In praise of overdrive, and seductive distortion
this album presents a sonic love affair with saturation, blown out harmonics and lethal subs.Texture is all…but the
result is bewilderingly more sensual than brutal.
Noise as medication and noise as salvation.. An avalanche of white noise buries submerged melodies, whilst relentless rib/speaker rattling low end frequencies ensure a full body massage is maintained throughout… If anything, this album resembles the huge sonic peaks of Martin's infamous live 'Sirens' shows most
This 10 track collection sounds absolutely massive, overflowing with oceanic swells of undulating riffs and deeply hypnotic pulsations combining to wash away non believers, as walls of drones are as stunning as they are threatening
Blasting off literally, with ‘Dreams of Flight’, which sounds like a dream date for Boards of Canada and Kevin Shields, it sets the seagazing tone for the rest of the album…
From Isolationist storm warnings to fictitious collisions between Flying Saucer Attack and Merzbow, the album
culminates in soothing electronic mantras for these perilous times.
This album is as bewitching as it is totally heavy….
Enjoy !...
Visual c/o Pedro Maia
PS Dedicated to Morisa aka Mrs Bug, the Queen of distortion, for whom my music is never distorted or heavy enough...Lol..An invaluable, inspiring partner in life.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released August 7, 2020
Written/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Visual c/o Pedro Maia

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Frequencies For Leaving Earth Vol.5
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
This is the final instalment of the ‘Frequencies for Leaving earth’ series.
The genesis of the 'Frequencies For Leaving Earth' concept was thought up by Kevin Martin in 2019.
When Martin subsequently took a long distance flight from Berlin to
Mexico City in Dec 2019, he wrote the skeletal sketches for ‘Volume 5’ during that journey. The smacked out jazz versus classical sirrup prototypes, were first composed on the plane at an altitude of 35,000 feet off the ground..…
Anyone previously smitten by The Bug vs Earth’s ‘Concrete Desert’, or the sick musical backdrop to King Midas Sound’s ‘Solitude’, could well fall for this long playing fade out to oblivion(or the Heavens,
take your pick..)…
At times sounding like William Basinski dropping ‘Lift to the scaffold’ at 5rpm, in other places resembling Coil jamming with Jimmy Garrison in the dystopian zone, these cavernous dubspaces meet seismic bass troughs, are continuously stalked by celestial drones.
Delirious and hypnotic..
Enjoy the trip
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released August 7, 2020
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Visuals c/o Pedro Maia

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Hazy
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
'Hazy' is a mini-album based on s singular sonic mood.
(So better look elsewhere if you want variation or blatant dynamics.)
An obsessively fixated selection of half heard imperfections, audio mirages and saturated speaker transmissions.
Collectively these 7 pieces form a langorously slow matrix of smudged melodies buried deep between a sea of white noise swells, where dense filtering is the only navigational tool...Perplexingly, the audible impression seems near and far... Sacred and haunting..At times sounding like a heavily sedated Marty Rev instrumental played at half speed, heard from an adjacent building. It remains strangely soothing, warm and soft centred.. fragile even..
Dont blame your ears, dont service your speakers,these smeared carousel compositions are best absorbed in a single sitting...
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released December 4, 2020
Composed / Produced : Kevin Richard Martin

Kevin Richard Martin.title: It's Time..
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
The duo’s first release since 2019’s jaw dropping ‘Solitude’ on Cosmo Rhythmatic, King Midas Sound return for an EP overflowing with dramatic tension and sonic turbulence. An eerie amalgamation of magic realism and haunting heavyweight audio, K.M.S. map out a hallucinatory zone where drones + riffs are memorably filtered into dream states for suspended animation addicts. Slow and low, blurred and delirious, the spectral tracks pass like disturbed fever dreams, as the pain of blind prejudice is surgically dissected by a gifted poet.
Lyrically, Roger Robinson celebrates his recent capture of the prestigious TS Eliot poetry award, with his most militantly direct prose yet for KMS. Balancing surrealism and horror in a master craftsman’s head and hand, the poems herein could well have been addendums to his incredible ‘Portable Paradise’ collection..……Whilst sub freq specialist The Bug, aka Kevin Martin, comes off the back of his recent 5 part solo lockdown series of recordings, ‘Frequencies for Leaving Earth’, with further magnetic probes into beatless, infinite, deep space.
If Don Cherry’s ‘Brown Rice’ was fused with Sunn 0))), it may resemble the goosebump inducing slo-mo dirge of ‘Gold’. Whilst alternately, ‘Hung’ sounds like Eliane Radigue’s minimalist tone theories, narrated by the ghosts of ‘Strange Fruit’…
Beauty is to be found compellingly, throughout the molten ambience of this mesmeric e.p.. Captivating it is…highly original too…Dive in…
Kevin Richard Martin.credits: released September 4, 2020

Kevin Richard Martin.title: KRM live dj session @meakusmaradiolab

Kevin Richard Martin.about:
My way of saying thank you to all those who purchased my 'Frequencies for leaving earth 1 - 5' series.
This is a free download recording taken of my first live dj set as Kevin Richard Martin. Recorded by the Meakusma Festival in Eupen/Belgium on 5th September 2020
The set predominantly features my own music, alongside the likes of Einsturzende Neubauten, Tim Hecker, Final, William Basinski, Teleplasmiste, King Krule, King Midas Sound and an exclusive premiere extract of a track from the forthcoming The Bug ft. Dis Fig album.
Pay nothing, and then immerse yourself.

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Melting Point
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Arguably the most beautifully immersive release yet from Kevin RIchard Martin. From underwater gamelans to liquified drone metallics, via submerged orchestras and vertical ambience, the mood remains lush, warm and eternal, as infinite loops fuse with perpetually shapeshifting micro melodies. Indeed the low pass filters are worked hard, as the spirit of dub transmits from the bottom of this oceanic sound.
At times, the album resembles Gavin Bryars re-scoring Dylan Carlson's Earth, or Sunn 0))) remixed by GAS man Wolfgang Voigt at his most smudged and dilated. But Martin's midas touch is instantly recognisable, as he continuously crafts tone and colour. He has let light in emphatically on 'Melting Point', but deceptively/ironicaly, its no less heavy. Distant bells and slow motion riffs permeate mind and body, with no focul point in earshot, as the expansive textures provide addictive ear candy throughout this bewitchingly slow internal voyage.
I pressed start and a week later i dont feel the need to press stop. Repetitions on repeat. Utterly enchanting.
Nigel Roberts 2021
Composed / Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Mixed : Brussels Zoo
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released August 6, 2021
Cover photograph : Vinicius "amnx" Amano

'Nightcrawler' is a cinematic, drone jazz odyssey.
A liquid flow of sax, double bass and analogue synths, hypnotically wrapped into a moody work of urban fiction.
Anyone who previously enjoyed the bad trip immersion into The Bug vs Earth's 'Concrete desert', or the outward bound trajectory of Kevin Richard Martin's - 'Return to Solaris', will find soul food within these bewitching, slow motion driftworks.

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Red Light
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Kevin RIchard Martin confesses he has "always had a love/hate relationshipwith the saxophone.". "After you hear Pharoah Sanders, John Coltrane, Albert Ayler or Peter Brotzmann even, you just question any possibility of finding a fresh sound or approach, due to the instant connotations the sound has.."
Having given up professionally playing the instrument since 'Curse of the Golden Vampire's 'Mass Destruction' in 2003, he may have temporarily lost the will to blow the horn, but he hadnt lost the long term aim to "reduce its sound to pure  gulf stream air" by other means.So for both 'White Light' and 'Red Light', Kevin decided to resample his old sax recordings, and additionally explore various sound libraries/vst instruments as the departure point, to see just how heavily the instrument could be chopped, screwed, stretched and dubbed in his lab, to try and locate his "own sound". Martin informs me enthusiastically, "there were no other sounds or sources utilised on these albums, other than saxophone or additionally synthetic sax, filtered through layer upon layer, wave upon wave of disorientating fx.
'Red Light' is the shadowy companion album to 'White Light'. More melancholic and arguably slower still, it contains shorter form compositions, and a moodier veneer... For anyone who was bewitched by The Bug's previous collaboration with Earth for 'Concrete Desert', this could  well hit the mark, as a resonantly foggy follow up to that L.A. inspired sprawling opus.
Film noir-jazz drugged down to a snail's pace, is duly dusted with Martin's signature atmospheric dub pressure, as the spirit of David Lynch and the ghosts of broken lives are invoked for 'Gone', Whilst it could be Art Ensemble of Chicago emulating Sunn 0)))'s slow jams, on the crawl out, end piece, for the aptly titled, 'Oblivion Seeker'....
A soundtrack to long nights in concrete jungles, the celluloid vision of 'Red Light' is sharp as a blade. Anyone who digs the idea of  hearing sax players float into the heavens, and jazz pulsations emerging from the titanic, are in for a treat...
Kevin Richard Martin.credits: released March 5, 2021

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Sedatives
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Like everyone staying afloat in this year with no head,,
Kevin Martin sought refuge from the madness.
By building beautiful loops as sonic sedatives, he constructed monastic psalms to battle the chaos triggered by the Co-vid pathogen.
Balletic,,hypnotic and revelling in the joy of drifting…this set of drone poems fully praise the analgesia of total sound immersion. Moving from solid to liquid to air, underwater jazz, iced ambient and mesmerically dubbed phase shifts, ooze both charm and melancholy. Casting irresistibly smeared frequency spells, left, right and centre, its a pleasure getting fully submerged in this quietly overwhelming album…
The relentlessly blurred undulations continuously filtered, throughout the twenty plus minutes of 20/20,
sounds like Maurizio meeting Gavin Bryars in the deepest pacific ocean trench, whilst Long Winter sounds like listening to ‘In a Silent Way’ in a wind tunnel on a snail paced, defective record deck with VERY large speaker boxes. Lastly, the final suite, ’Refractions 1 - 5’, deconstructs a dream fusion for AR Kane and Eno, adding dream machinery to the outward bound vibrations. Time is immaculately slowed down, reversed and tricked, within a sea of overdriven fx in Martin’s echo chamber…
Having only recently discovered KRM’s work via ‘Frequencies For Leaving Earth Vol.1’, its a pleasure to get sucked ever deeper, in to this K-hole. 'Sedatives' as a collection of nebulous musical mirages, offers fading, impressionistic water colours as rays of light in these dark times.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released November 6, 2020
Music Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Artwork : Simon Fowler

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Sub Zero
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
"Just when you thought Kevin Richard Martin's music couldnt go any slower, lower or deeper, 'Sub Zero' emerges.
This slow motion, drug-tech, dream noise, dub excavation, into
hypnotic pulsations and melodic melancholia.
is arguably Martin's most striking release yet on his own Intercranial Recordings imprint.
Unnervingly, the album is as beautiful as it is
solemn, as glacial as it is relentless, and as subtle as it is terrifying. A trip into a sonic abyss, with a tour of a philosophical void, its to my ears, the most seductive yet for KRM, and also the most resonanantly moving, as he expertly balances tearjerk motifs with heavier than
hell, rhythmic weight.
With its melodic fog, eternal drones and eerie atmospherics, the peripheral throb of distant kick drums, the heartbeat punctuation of cavernous subs and the snowstorm blizzard of fuzz absolutely envelopes the mind, whilst crushing the soul.
If you look for precedents, this album maybe echoes a more paranoid Porter ricks, a dystopian GAS, or even a dubbed to f-ck Pansonic. Yet perhaps most apt, is it seems to go further into the deepest dubspace Martin explored on 'In Blue', his recent collab with Dis Fig for Hyperdub, as The Bug..."
A.Carver. London. Feb 2022
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released March 4, 2022
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Mixed : Brussels Zoo
Photo c/o Tom Morel / UNSPLASH

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Sub Zero (2026 Remaster)
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Just when you thought Kevin Richard Martin's music couldn’t go any slower, lower or deeper, Sub Zero emerges. A slow-motion excavation of drug-tech, dub, dreamy noise and frozen ambience, the album gradually mutates into hypnotic pulsations and melodic melancholia. It is arguably Martin’s most striking release to date under his given name.
Originally released digitally on Bandcamp only in the depths of winter 2022, amid the final year of the COVID-19 pandemic and Russia’s initial invasion of Ukraine, this desolate epic went on to become KRM's best-selling digital album on the platform. With persistent demand for a vinyl pressing and a full DSP release from fans, Martin thought the time was right for Sub Zero to finally surface in its full glory: remastered and paired with fresh new artwork.
The LP will be fully released on Feb 27th 2026.
(Pre sold from 16/01/2026, via Boomkat, Bleep, Norman Records etc)
Unnervingly, the album is as beautiful as it is solemn, as glacial as it is relentless, and as subtle as it is terrifying. A trip into a sonic abyss, with a tour of a philosophical void, it’s to my ears, KRM’s most seductive work yet, and also his most emotionally resonant. Martin expertly balances tear-jerking motifs with heavier than hell rhythmic weight. With its melodic fog, eternal drones and eerie atmospherics, the peripheral throb of distant kick drums, the heartbeat punctuation of cavernous subs and the snowstorm blizzard of fuzz absolutely envelopes the mind, whilst crushing the soul.
In terms of lineage, Sub Zero might recall a more paranoid Porter Ricks, a dystopian GAS, or a brutally dubbed-out Pan Sonic. Most fitting, however, is its kinship with the deepest dub terrain Martin previously explored on In Blue, The Bug’s acclaimed 2020 collaboration with Dis Fig for Hyperdub, where he obsessively probed subaqueous pulses and low-end modulations.
Sub Zero is possibly the most minimal, desolate, and deviant dub record yet released on Martin’s PRESSURE label. It marks the point at which dub disappears into its own effects trails. Dub music capturing frozen moments in time. Dub as an addictive painkiller, that sounds both sacred and ocean deep.
The album will be followed by a remix 12”, featuring reworks from Ghost Dubs and The Bug, arriving in Spring 2026.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released January 16, 2026
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin
Mixed : Brussels Zoo
Cover Photo : The Pathological Puppy
Mastered : Frederic Alstadt
Press Release : Ken Li

Kevin Richard Martin.title: White Light
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
Kevin RIchard Martin confesses he has "always had a love/hate relationshipwith the saxophone.". "After you hear Pharoah Sanders, John Coltrane, Albert Ayler or Peter Brotzmann even, you just question any possibility of finding a fresh sound or approach, due to the instant connotations the sound has.."
Having given up professionally playing the instrument since 'Curse of the Golden Vampire's 'Mass Destruction' in 2003, he may have temporarily lost the will to blow the horn, but he hadnt lost the long term aim to "reduce its sound to pure  gulf stream air" by other means. So for both 'White Light' and 'Red Light', Kevin decided to resample his old sax recordings, and additionally explore various sound libraries/vst instruments as the departure point, to see just how heavily the instrument could be chopped, screwed, stretched and dubbed in his lab, to try and locate his "own sound". Martin informs me enthusiastically, "there were no other sounds or sources utilised on these albums, other than saxophone or additionally, specifically synthetic sax, filtered through layer upon layer, wave upon wave of fx."
'White Light' contains the long form results of these deconstruction sessions. The prolonged "meltdown cycles", spectral solos and hypnotically evolving loops sound as eerie as they do beautiful, as the liquid brass is set adrift in the studio's magical domain.  The indelible inspiration of Techno Animal's 'Dream Forger' from 1991, side 2 of Bowie/Eno's 'Low' and years of "getting truly lost in dub" evidently results here in some of the most captivating music he has ever produced. Opener, ''Into Air' resembles Burial remixing 'DIsintegration Loops' with a solo to die for, whilst 'Float away' echoes the Arkestra recorded underwater at 11rpm. Elsewhere liquid drone and classical drift converge to form radiant rivers of fluid, blissed out brass. If the King Midas Sound's collab with Fennesz for 'Edition 1'(Ninja Tune) was Kevin's ambient starting point, this album has reached the next stunning level of dreamy evolution.
KRM has delivered a slow motion Jazz anesthetic to kill all pain.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released March 5, 2021
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin

trackinfo.credits:
from White Light, released March 5, 2021
Composed/Produced : Kevin Richard Martin

Kevin Richard Martin.title: Silent
Kevin Richard Martin.label: Pressure
Kevin Richard Martin.about:
'Silent' is an 11min epic, A broken ballad, where slo-mo jazz meets deeply frozen ambience, with Dis Fig's resonantly blue melancholic croon imploding gracefully...
Loss, fragility, and an eerie, glacial beauty are on full display, with it's naked emotional impact...
A singular single.
Dis Fig / Martin decided to specifically release 'Silent' specifically on Valentine's Day 2025, and the former recorded her vocals on a reworked version of 'Black'(the title track of KRM's album of the same name which dropped in 2024), during the last week, for Martin to mix/produce it in time for Feb 14th.
Kevin Richard Martin.credits:
released February 14, 2025
Composed / Produced : Kevin Richard Martin (Brussels Zoo)
Lyrics / Vocals : Dis Fig aka Felicia Chen
Written : Kevin Richard Martin / DIs Fig
Mastered : Frederic Alstadt Mont Analogue
KRM published by Third Side Music
Dis Fig Copyright Control 2025
Copyright - Intercranial Recordings 2025
